perturbation.original_text: Vector agrees that, for a period of three (3) months from the termination or expiration of this Agreement, unless such shall have been specifically invited in writing by SafeNet, neither Vector nor any of the investment funds it manages will in any manner, directly or indirectly: (a) effect to seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect, or cause or participate in or in any way assist any other person to effect or seek, offer or propose (whether publicly or otherwise) to effect or participate in, (i) any acquisition of any securities (or beneficial ownership thereof) or assets of SafeNet; (ii) any tender or exchange offer, merger or other business combination involving SafeNet; (iii) any recapitalization, restructuring, liquidation, dissolution or other extraordinary transaction with respect to SafeNet; or (iv) any “solicitation” or “proxies” (as such terms are used in the proxy rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission) or consents to vote any voting securities of SafeNet; (b) form, join or in any way participate in a “group” (as defined under the Securities Exchange Act) relating to SafeNet or its securities; (c) otherwise act, alone or in concert. with others, to seek to control or influence the management, Board of Directors or policies of SafeNet; (d) take any action which might force SafeNet to make a public announcement regarding any of the types of matters set forth in (a) above; or (e) enter into any discussions or arrangements with any third party with respect to any of the foregoing. You also agree during such period not to request SafeNet (or its directors, officers, employees or agents), directly or indirectly, to amend or waive any provision of this Section (including this sentence). Notwithstanding the foregoing, nothing herein shall limit Vector’s ability to acquire up to l% of the SafeNet’s equity securities, subject only to full compliance with all applicable securities laws and regulations.
perturbation.changed_text: Vector agrees to comply with the restrictions outlined in Section 4(a)-(e) following the termination or expiration of this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, nothing herein shall limit Vector’s ability to acquire up to l% of the SafeNet’s equity securities, subject only to full compliance with all applicable securities laws and regulations.
perturbation.explanation: By omitting the specific period of three (3) months for Vector's restrictions after termination/expiration, and other details of (a)-(e), but retaining the section's heading and referencing compliance with the restrictions, a contradiction is created. It's unclear how long the restrictions last or their precise scope, creating uncertainty in enforcement because the duration of the restriction, has been completely omited.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Vector agrees to comply with the restrictions outlined in Section 4(a)-(e) following the termination or expiration of this Agreement. Notwithstanding the foregoing, nothing herein shall limit Vector’s ability to acquire up to l% of the SafeNet’s equity securities, subject only to full compliance with all applicable securities laws and regulations.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies a three-month restriction period following termination or expiration, along with detailed restrictions (a)-(e). The changed text omits the three-month period and the specifics of restrictions (a)-(e), only stating that Vector agrees to comply with Section 4(a)-(e). The contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text, relies on the content of Section 4(a)-(e) for its meaning. Since the specifics of (a)-(e) and the three-month period are removed from the changed text, the contradicted text now lacks the necessary context for a reasonable understanding of the obligations. This is an Omission-based contradiction because the removal of essential language creates a conflict with the referenced but undefined section (4(a)-(e)), leading to ambiguity and uncertainty in enforcement, as the duration and precise scope of the restrictions are no longer clear.

perturbation.original_text: The Disclosing Party warrants that it has the right to possession and disclosure of the Information according to the terms of this Agreement. Each Party understands and acknowledges 2 that neither Party has made any representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Information and that neither Party shall have any liability to the other or any other person resulting from reliance on the Information. Only those representations or warranties that are made in a definitive agreement, and subject to such limitations and restrictions as may be specified therein, will have any legal effect.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party understands and acknowledges that neither Party has made any representation or warranty, express or implied, as to the accuracy or completeness of the Information and that neither Party shall have any liability to the other or any other person resulting from reliance on the Information. Only those representations or warranties that are made in a definitive agreement, and subject to such limitations and restrictions as may be specified therein, will have any legal effect.
perturbation.explanation: The omission of 'The Disclosing Party warrants that it has the right to possession and disclosure of the Information according to the terms of this Agreement' removes a key warranty. The contradiction arises because later sections assume this warranty exists, particularly concerning liability for improper disclosure. The receiving party now has no assurance that the disclosing party actually *can* legally disclose the information which creates risk.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the event that a Receiving Party is requested pursuant to, or required by, applicable law, regulation or legal process to disclose any of the Disclosing Party’s Information, the Receiving Party will notify the Disclosing Party promptly before such Information is disclosed so that the Disclosing Party may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy or, in the sole reasonable discretion of the Disclosing Party, waive compliance with the terms of this Agreement. In the event that no such protective order or other remedy is obtained, or that such Disclosing Party waives compliance with the terms of this Agreement, the Receiving Party will furnish only that portion of such Information which it is advised in writing by outside counsel is legally required and will exercise its best efforts to obtain reliable assurance that confidential treatment will be accorded such Information. Disclosure of information in accordance with this Section shall not be considered a breach of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The omission of 'The Disclosing Party warrants that it has the right to possession and disclosure of the Information according to the terms of this Agreement' in the changed text creates a contradiction with the contradicted text. The contradicted text outlines the procedure for handling legally required disclosures of the Disclosing Party’s Information by the Receiving Party. However, without the warranty from the original text ensuring the Disclosing Party's right to possession and disclosure, the Receiving Party now has no assurance about the legality of the initial disclosure itself. The contradicted section assumes the disclosing party is entitled to disclose the information, but that right is now removed, which creates a risk and thus a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party represents that, as of the date of this agreement, neither Party nor its controlled affiliates, nor anyone acting on its or their behalf in connection with the Transaction, has acquired record or beneficial ownership of any voting securities of the other Party or of any securities convertible or exchangeable into or exercisable for voting securities of the other Party, and each Party agrees that neither it, nor any of its directors, officers or employees who have been provided with the Information will perform any purchase, sale or other transfer of any of the securities of the other Party in violation of United States securities laws.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party agrees that neither it, nor any of its directors, officers or employees who have been provided with the Information will perform any purchase, sale or other transfer of any of the securities of the other Party in violation of United States securities laws.
perturbation.explanation: Removing the representation that 'as of the date of this agreement, neither Party...has acquired record or beneficial ownership of any voting securities...' creates an in-text contradiction. Without this baseline, it is impossible to determine whether subsequent purchases or sales violate US securities laws, because it's unknown if pre-existing ownership existed. Section 6 now lacks a defined starting point for compliance.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party agrees that neither it, nor any of its directors, officers or employees who have been provided with the Information will perform any purchase, sale or other transfer of any of the securities of the other Party in violation of United States securities laws.
perturbation.justification: The original text establishes a baseline regarding ownership of securities. The changed text removes this baseline. The contradicted text describes an agreement not to violate US securities laws regarding the purchase, sale or transfer of securities. Without the baseline of the original text, it's impossible to determine whether subsequent actions violate securities laws. The absence of this initial representation introduces a contradiction regarding how compliance with securities laws is assessed, as the 'starting point' has been removed.

perturbation.original_text: The Receiving Party shall have, or shall enter into, agreements with its parent, divisions and subsidiary companies as necessary to safeguard the Information disclosed hereunder consistent with the terms of this Agreement. With respect to its directors, officers and employees, the Receiving Party warrants that it has executed confidentiality agreements with all such directors, officers and employees who will have access to Information, and shall ensure that such directors, officers and employees protect Information according to the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: The Receiving Party shall have, or shall enter into, agreements with its parent, divisions and subsidiary companies as necessary to safeguard the Information disclosed hereunder consistent with the terms of this Agreement. With respect to its directors, officers and employees, the Receiving Party shall ensure that such directors, officers and employees protect Information according to the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Omitting 'the Receiving Party warrants that it has executed confidentiality agreements with all such directors, officers and employees who will have access to Information' introduces a contradiction. It's now unclear if confidentiality agreements are actually in place, weakening the protection of 'Information' and potentially negating the effectiveness of later legal actions based on breaches of confidentiality.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party acknowledges that remedies at law may be inadequate to protect it against any actual or threatened breach of this Agreement by the other Party, and, without prejudice to any other rights and remedies otherwise available to each Party, each Party agrees to any grant of injunctive relief in favor of the other Party without proof of actual damages. In the event of litigation relating to this Agreement, if a court of competent jurisdiction determines that this 3 Agreement has been breached by a Party, then the breaching Party will reimburse the non-breaching Party for its costs and expenses (including, without limitation, legal fees and expenses) incurred in connection with all such litigation.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that the Receiving Party warrants it has executed confidentiality agreements with its directors, officers, and employees. The changed text omits this warranty, creating uncertainty about whether these agreements are in place. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which assumes the agreement's terms are enforceable, because without these agreements, remedies at law may be inadequate, and the basis for seeking injunctive relief is weakened, creating a contradiction in enforceability.

perturbation.original_text: Either Party may terminate this Agreement at will by written notice to the other Party and, if not terminated earlier, shall terminate automatically one (1) year from the Effective Date, except the obligations of use and confidentiality pursuant to Sections 2 shall continue for a period of two (2) years from the Effective Date,
perturbation.changed_text: Either Party may terminate this Agreement at will by written notice to the other Party and, if not terminated earlier, shall terminate automatically one (1) year from the Effective Date,
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'except the obligations of use and confidentiality pursuant to Sections 2 shall continue for a period of two (2) years from the Effective Date', the agreement's termination clause now contradicts the implicit expectation that confidentiality continues beyond the initial term. This means that section 2, where it talks about confidentiality of the parties, now does not hold after a year, contradicting what the section is trying to convey to the parties on when is not appropriate to disclose any kind of Information
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Receiving Party agrees that; (a) it will use Information disclosed by the Disclosing Party solely for the purpose of discussing and evaluating the Transaction, and for no other purpose; 1 (b) it will not distribute, disclose or disseminate Information to any third Party without the prior consent of the Disclosing Party, and will only distribute, disclose or disseminate Information to its directors, officers, employees consultants, advisors, potential sources of financing or other representatives with a need to know such information for purposes of discussing and evaluating the Transaction and who are informed by such Receiving Party of the confidential nature of the Information; (c) it will protect the Information of the Disclosing Part), against unauthorized disclosure and warrants that will use the same means to protect Information that it uses to protect its own confidential information, in any event no less than reasonable means.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly states that confidentiality obligations in Section 2 continue for two years, even after the agreement terminates after one year. The changed text removes this clause, implying that confidentiality obligations cease after one year. This creates a direct contradiction with Section 2, which outlines ongoing confidentiality responsibilities, making them unenforceable after the first year, as the termination clause no longer carves out this exception.
